summary: Audited 9 core files + autoloads for function length, file size, _process efficiency, orphan nodes, and hot-path allocations. 7 new issues found, 4 PASS patterns noted.

findings.detail: 2951 lines — 7.4x above 400-line rule. Urgent split needed into: card_generator, calendar_adapter, swarm_coordinator, guardrails_engine.
2236 lines — 5.6x above 400-line rule. Split candidates: brain_router, backend_manager, process_poll_handler.
_apply_offering() is 184 lines — 3.7x above 50-line rule. Split into _apply_offering_life(), _apply_offering_faction(), _apply_offering_ogham().
start_run() is 136 lines — 2.7x above limit. Delegates to store but mixes setup, signal wiring, and UI init.
10x RegEx.new() called inside sanitizer functions. Allocates/compiles regex on every LLM response. Should be precompiled static class-level constants.
Time.get_time_dict_from_system() called every frame in _process() to check for minute change. Should use a 10s Timer to throttle to 6x/min instead of 3600x/min.
print() on every WebSocket event (connect, data, send) inside _process(). Fine for dev/debug but should be behind OS.is_debug_build() guard for release.

passes.detail: _process() uses correct early-return pattern (is_empty check). No allocations in hot path.
_process() is a clean state machine dispatch. Minimal work per frame.
set_process(false) correctly disabled at init, only enabled when background tasks are active. Efficient.
_process() does only arithmetic, period comparison, signal emit. No allocations. Correct.

task_priority_note: Implementing LLM-RAG-SUBSTR-FIX (P18) + LLM-RAG-CONTEXT-ROLE (P19) this cycle — same file, same fix, both closed.
patterns_learned: RegEx.new() inside frequently-called functions = hidden allocation per call — always precompile
Time.get_time_dict_from_system() per frame = unnecessary system call — use Timer for periodic checks
PERF violations in test files (>400 lines) are acceptable — test files exempt from size rule
